prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French: Le silence entre nous dit plus que tout argument aurait pu le faire.

Japanese: その沈黙が私たち之间よりも何かを語った。

Arabic: الصمت بيننا قال أكثر مما يمكن أية جدال أن يفعل.

The phrase "said more than any argument could have" was rendered a bit differently in each translation. In French, I translated it as "dit plus que tout argument aurait pu le faire," while in Japanese, I rendered it as "その沈黙が私たち之间よりも何かを語った" (that silence between us said more than any argument could have). In Arabic, I translated it as "الصمت بيننا قال أكثر مما يمكن أية جدال أن يفعل" (the silence between us said more than any argument could have). I chose to keep a slightly different phrasing to best capture the idiomatic expression in each language while still preserving the core idea of silence being more eloquent than arguments. None of these phrases posed particular difficulty in translation.
